note: Rule summaries are original paraphrases for tooling purposes. Consult the official BARR-C:2018 standard (barrgroup.com) for normative wording.

1.1a.summary: Code shall compile cleanly at the compiler's strictest warning level.
1.1a.fix: Enable -Wall -Wextra -Werror (or equivalent) and eliminate every warning.

1.3a.summary: Braces shall always surround the bodies of if, else, switch, while, do and for statements.
1.3a.fix: Add braces to every controlled block, even single statements.

1.7a.summary: The goto, continue and break-out-of-nested-loop patterns are restricted; goto shall not be used.
1.7a.fix: Restructure control flow with loops and status flags.

1.7c.summary: Comparisons shall not test the result of an assignment.
1.7c.fix: Assign first, compare on the next line.

2.1a.summary: Only /* ... */ and // comment styles are permitted; comments shall not be nested.
2.1a.fix: Normalize comment style and remove nesting.

2.2.summary: Code shall never be commented out; use version control or #if 0 with an explanatory comment only temporarily.
2.2.fix: Delete dead code blocks living in comments.

3.1a.summary: Lines shall be limited to a maximum width (80 characters) for review and diff readability.
3.1a.fix: Wrap long lines at logical boundaries.

3.2a.summary: Indentation shall use spaces, not tab characters, so alignment is identical in every tool.
3.2a.fix: Convert tabs to spaces per the project indent width.

4.2a.summary: Each module shall have a header (.h) exposing only its public interface.
4.2a.fix: Split public API into a header; keep internals static in the .c file.

5.2a.summary: Fixed-width integer types from <stdint.h> shall be used instead of char/short/int/long for numeric data.
5.2a.fix: Replace basic types with int8_t..uint64_t as appropriate.

5.4a.summary: Numeric literal suffixes shall be uppercase (U, L, UL) to prevent misreading.

5.6a.summary: Dynamic heap allocation shall be avoided in embedded firmware after initialization.
5.6a.fix: Allocate statically or from init-time pools only.

6.1a.summary: Functions shall not be recursive, directly or indirectly.
6.1a.fix: Rewrite recursive algorithms iteratively with bounded storage.

6.2a.summary: Functions used only within one module shall be declared static.

6.3a.summary: Function length should stay reviewable (fits on roughly one printed page, ~100 lines).

7.1e.summary: Identifier naming: no identifiers that differ only by case, and no leading underscores (reserved).
7.1e.fix: Rename identifiers to unambiguous, non-reserved names.

8.2a.summary: Conditional expressions shall be explicit comparisons rather than relying on implicit truthiness of assignments.

8.3a.summary: Pointer tests shall compare explicitly against NULL.

8.5a.summary: Every switch shall end with a default case that traps unexpected values.
8.5a.fix: Add a default branch with error handling or an assertion.

8.6a.summary: Loops shall have provably bounded termination (no unbounded while(1) without exit intent documented).
8.6a.fix: Bound loops with counters/timeouts; document intentional scheduler loops.
